rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOVIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One (1) And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 11, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One (1) And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, set one, is denied. There do not appear to be any further responses that the Court can compel Defendant CCSF to make to Plaintiff's form interrogatories. Despite lodging some objections that may have been unnecessary, there is nothing improper about answering the interrogatories with reference to other documents or information previously provided to Plaintiff. Accordingly, Plaintiff's request for sanctions is also denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Station House C1 Et Al S Amended Notice Of Hearing On General And Special Demurrers To Plaintiffs Superseding Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2019, Line 16, DEFENDANT VC MULTIFAMILY PORTFOLIO VENTURE GP, LLC, STATION HOUSE C1, LLC, VC FUND C-1, LLC Defendants Station House C1 Et Al S Amended Notice Of Hearing On General And Special Demurrers To Plaintiffs Superseding Third Amended Complaint Defendants Station House C1, LLC, VC Multifamily Portfolio Venture GP, LLC and VC Fund C-1, LLC's demurrer to the first, second and fourth causes of action in the superseding third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Mint Collection - 410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Plaintiff Owners' Association has not alleged, nor does it appear that it is able to allege, ultimate facts showing that any of the wrongdoing allegedly committed by the demurring defendants (as opposed to other defendants) caused any damage or harm to the common areas of either of the two buildings, the only areas of the buildings that plaintiff has standing to sue. Despite being given an opportunity to specifically identify any damage or harm to any common areas that is attributable to the demurring defendants, plaintiff has not done so and it appears that it is unable to do so. Grouping all builder defendants together without differentiation among them or explaining why they are all liable for the exact same damage or harm despite their different roles with regard to the buildings does not suffice to state a claim against the demurring defendants, particularly since this case has been pending for over two years. Counsel for the demurring defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2019, Line 16, DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. AMENDED DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Veritas Investment, Inc.'s demurrer to the first, second and fourth causes of action in the superseding third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Mint Collection - 410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Plaintiff Owners' Association has not alleged, nor does it appear that it is able to allege, ultimate facts showing that any of the wrongdoing allegedly committed by defendant Station House C1, LLC, Veritas' alleged alter ego, caused any damage or harm to the common areas of either of the two buildings, the only areas of the building that plaintiff has standing to sue. Despite being given an opportunity to specifically identify any damage or harm to any common areas that is attributable to Station House, plaintiff has not done so and it appears that it is unable to do so. Grouping all builder defendants together without differentiation among them or explaining why they are all liable for the exact same damage or harm despite their different roles with regard to the buildings does not suffice to allege actionable wrongdoing against Station House, particularly since this case has been pending for over two years. Counsel for Veritas is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JASON PEREZ VS. ERIC P. SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JASON PEREZ MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint Plaintiff Jason Perez's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted on specified conditions which counsel for Mr. Perez must agree to either orally at the hearing or in writing sent to counsel for defendants Eric Smith and Berman Skin Institute Medical Group, Inc. prior to the hearing. The conditions that Mr. Perez must agree to are: 1) after the first amended complaint is filed, defendants may withdraw their current motion for summary judgment and file one or more motions for summary judgment and/or adjudication on 30 days instead of 75 days' notice; 2) Mr. Perez must serve verified code-compliant responses without objection other than privilege no later than February 21, 2019 to the following deem-served discovery: a) state all facts in support of his new claim for negligent hiring, supervision and retention; b) state the name and contact information for each person who knows any of those facts and what facts each person knows; c) identify all documents which refer to or memorialize all such facts and produce them. If Mr. Perez does not agree to all of these conditions, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Perez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and states whether Mr. Perez does or does not contest to the conditions and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Defendant shall bring copies for all parties and the Court of Ms. Fairbrother's declarations, along with all exhibits attached to the declarations. rulings.Case Title: THERMO ENERGY SYSTEMS, INC. VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2019, Line 17, Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction Plaintiff Thermo Energy Systems, Inc.'s motion for a preliminary injunction enjoining defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A from disposing in any way with the money deposited into Wells Fargo Bank accounts xxxxxx4046 and xxxxxx4826 until entry of final judgment in this action is granted. The motion is unopposed and Thermo Energy has shown that it is likely to prevail on the merits of its fraud claims against Doe defendants based on evidence that Thermo Energy has been the victim of a fraudulent phishing scheme. (Collard Decl. Exh. A-E 12). Thermo Energy has also shown that it is likely to suffer irreparable harm because of likelihood that absent a preliminary injunction as a result of the money that likely will be withdrawn from the accounts and dissipated before the entry of judgment. Wells Fargo Bank and its officers, employees, agents, contractors, and representatives, and all entities acting by or through them, including trustees, are enjoined from directly or indirectly doing or attempting to proceed with any transfer, disposal, removal, withdrawal or any other action whatsoever with respect to the monies deposited in Wells Fargo Bank accounts xxxxxx4046 and xxxxxx4826 until entry of final judgment in this action. Per CCP 529 Thermo Energy is required to post a bond in the amount of $10,000.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Thermo Energy is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
